questions.question: Solve |x - 5| = 12.
questions.explanation: Absolute value equation splits into two cases: x - 5 = 12 gives x = 17, and x - 5 = -12 gives x = -7. Both are valid solutions.

questions.question: Solve |3x + 2| < 8.
questions.explanation: For |A| < 8, we get -8 < 3x + 2 < 8. Subtract 2: -10 < 3x < 6. Divide by 3: -10/3 < x < 2.

questions.question: A manufacturing machine produces bolts with length 10 cm. The tolerance allows bolts to be within |length - 10| ≤ 0.3 cm. What is the acceptable range of bolt lengths?
questions.explanation: The inequality |length - 10| ≤ 0.3 means -0.3 ≤ length - 10 ≤ 0.3. Adding 10 to all parts gives the range 9.7 to 10.3 cm.

questions.question: Solve |2x - 4| = |x + 3|.
questions.explanation: When absolute values are equal, either 2x - 4 = x + 3 (giving x = 7) or 2x - 4 = -(x + 3) (giving 3x = 1, so x = 1/3). Wait, let me recalculate: 2x - 4 = -x - 3 gives 3x = 1, so x = 1/3. Check: |2(1/3) - 4| = |2/3 - 4| = 10/3, and |1/3 + 3| = 10/3. ✓ And x = 7: |14 - 4| = 10, |7 + 3| = 10. ✓

questions.question: For what values of x is |x + 4| ≥ 6?
questions.explanation: When |A| ≥ 6, we get A ≤ -6 or A ≥ 6. So x + 4 ≤ -6 gives x ≤ -10, and x + 4 ≥ 6 gives x ≥ 2.

questions.question: Solve |5 - 2x| = 9. Give the solution with the larger value first.
questions.explanation: Case 1: 5 - 2x = 9 gives -2x = 4, so x = -2. Case 2: 5 - 2x = -9 gives -2x = -14, so x = 7. The larger solution is x = 7.
